narrative_summary: The engine compared expected and actual outcomes, then mapped the biggest deltas to recurring audience, creative, and platform signals. Positive metric movement is treated as a repeatable pattern candidate, while cost inflation or missing revenue is flagged for follow-up.
key_learnings: 18-25 audiences drive a 54% stronger performance score versus baseline, with 2% lower CVR and 49% lower CPA across 1 campaigns.
18-30 audiences drive a 54% stronger performance score versus baseline, with 2% lower CVR and 49% lower CPA across 1 campaigns.
Image creatives deliver a 54% stronger performance score, 2% lower CVR, and 49% lower CPA while sustaining 3.5% CTR across 1 campaigns.
Video creatives deliver a 54% stronger performance score, 2% lower CVR, and 49% lower CPA while sustaining 3.5% CTR across 1 campaigns.
recommendations: Expand tests around `audience:18-25` and preserve the audience framing.
Expand tests around `audience:18-30` and preserve the audience framing.
Tighten budget guardrails and require evidence before scaling this pattern.
Reuse the playbook from `cmp_001_meta_genz_video_launch` because it is the nearest semantic match in memory.
anomalies: CPA worsened by 58.0%, which indicates spend efficiency drift.
cmp_002_google_search_saas_demo is overspending relative to history, with CPA at 67.71 on 6500.00 spend while only converting at 4.2%.
